# 2026-04-20 05:50:37Z

<!-- cursoride Session 73544879-32f7-40eb-9f01-6c7f11a68faa (2026-04-20 05:50:37Z) -->

_**User**_

I want to integrate custom fonts into my React Native project using the `react-native-google-fonts` package.

Requirements:

1. Use Google Fonts:

   * Manrope
   * Inter

2. Configure these fonts inside the existing `typography.js` file.

3. Define a proper typography system including:

   * font families
   * font weights (regular, medium, bold, etc.)
   * reusable text styles (heading, subheading, body, caption, etc.)

4. Ensure that:

   * Fonts are loaded correctly using `react-native-google-fonts`
   * Typography styles are reusable across the project
   * The font system is applied globally (all screens/components should use this typography setup)

5. Update or create a centralized typography configuration so that:

   * No inline font styles are used
   * All components import and use styles from `typography.js`

6. Provide example usage in a sample component showing how to use the typography styles.

7. Make sure the implementation is clean, scalable, and follows best practices for React Native.

Output:

* Updated `typography.js`
* Font loading setup
* Example component using the typography system
in react native cli project

---

      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [@expo-google-fonts/manrope](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@expo-google-fonts/manrope)\n2. [@expo-google-fonts/inter](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@expo-google-fonts/inter)\n3. [@expo-google-fonts/manrope](https://registry.npmjs.org/%40expo-google-fonts%2Fmanrope)\n4. [google-fonts/font-packages/manrope at main · expo/google-fonts · GitHub](https://github.com/expo/google-fonts/tree/main/font-packages/manrope)\n5. [google-fonts/font-packages/manrope at main · expo/google-fonts · GitHub](https://github.com/expo/google-fonts/tree/master/font-packages/manrope)\n\n# Expo Google Fonts Packages\n\nThese are Expo packages (not react-native-google-fonts) that provide Google Fonts for Expo apps:\n\n## @expo-google-fonts/manrope[1]\n- Install: &#96;npm i @expo-google-fonts/manrope&#96;\n- Contains 7 font weights: ExtraLight, Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold\n- 22.9K weekly downloads\n- Size: 1.3MB unpacked\n\n## @expo-google-fonts/inter[2]\n- Install: &#96;npm i @expo-google-fonts/inter&#96;\n- Contains 18 styles (9 weights + italic variants): Thin through Black, each with italic options\n- 321.2K weekly downloads (significantly more popular)\n- Size: 7.7MB unpacked\n\n## Usage\nBoth packages follow the same pattern:[1][2]\n1. Install with &#96;npx expo install @expo-google-fonts/[font-name] expo-font&#96;\n2. Import the &#96;useFonts&#96; hook and desired font weights\n3. Load fonts with &#96;useFonts()&#96; before rendering\n4. Reference fonts by their exported names (e.g., &#96;fontFamily: \"Manrope_400Regular\"&#96;)\n\nBoth are maintained by the Expo Team and use MIT and OFL-1.1 licenses.[1][2]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: react-native-google-fonts npm package Manrope Inter"
    }
  ],

[GitHub - expo/google-fonts: Use any of the 1000+ fonts (and their variants) from fonts.google.com in your Expo app. · GitHub](https://github.com/expo/google-fonts/tree/master)\n\n# react-native-google-fonts Package Summary\n\n**react-native-google-fonts** is an npm package that allows you to use Google Fonts in your Expo app.[1]\n\n## Key Details\n\n- **Version**: 0.0.0 (published May 2, 2020)[1]\n- **Author**: Expo Team[1]\n- **License**: MIT[1]\n- **Weekly Downloads**: 136[1]\n- **Dependency**: expo-google-fonts[1]\n\n## Functionality\n\nThe package provides access to over 977 fonts (and their variants) from fonts.google.com for use in Expo applications.[1] The package works across web, iOS, and Android platforms.[2]\n\n## Installation\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nnpm i react-native-google-fonts\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Related Project\n\nThis package is part of the larger **expo/google-fonts** project on GitHub, which offers access to 1935 fonts and 7639 variants from Google Fonts.[2] The more commonly used approach involves installing specific font family packages like &#96;@expo-google-fonts/[font-family]&#96; along with the &#96;expo-font&#96; package.[2]\n\n**Note**: The npm package page shows minimal documentation (\"No README data found\"),[1] so users may want to refer to the GitHub repository for more detailed usage information.",

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Installing expo modules**

I'm installing expo modules via `npx install-expo-modules@latest` for bare React Native. I'm installing expo-font and @expo-google-fonts/manrope, @expo-google-fonts/inter for Google Fonts support. I'm running `npx install-expo-modules@latest` to prepare for bare workflow font loading. 

**Handling font weights**

I'm adjusting typography to use distinct fontFamily strings per weight instead of fontWeight for Android compatibility. I'm using distinct fontFamily strings per weight for Android compatibility. I'm referencing the exact fontFamily names from src/theme/typography.ts for consistency. I'm implementing a custom AppText component to manage global text styles consistently. </details></think>

[expo-font - Expo](https://www.mintlify.com/expo/expo/sdk/font)\n\n# Installing Expo Modules in Bare React Native with expo-font and useFonts\n\n## Installing Expo Modules\n\nTo use Expo modules in a bare React Native project, start by installing the &#96;expo&#96; package.[1] You can do this automatically or manually:\n\n**Automatic Installation:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sh\nnpx install-expo-modules@latest\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Manual Installation:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sh\nnpm install expo\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen configure Expo modules in your native code (Android and iOS) following the platform-specific instructions in the Expo documentation.[1]\n\n## Using expo-font with useFonts Hook\n\nInstall the &#96;expo-font&#96; library:[2]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sh\nnpx expo install expo-font\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe &#96;useFonts&#96; hook loads custom fonts at runtime and returns a &#96;[loaded, error]&#96; tuple:[5]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { useFonts } from 'expo-font';\nimport { Text } from 'react-native';\n\nfunction App() {\n  const [loaded, error] = useFonts({\n    'Inter-Black': require('./assets/fonts/Inter-Black.otf'),\n    'Inter-Regular': require('./assets/fonts/Inter-Regular.otf'),\n  });\n\n  if (!loaded \u0026\u0026 !error) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  return \u003cText style={{ fontFamily: 'Inter-Black' }}\u003eHello\u003c/Text\u003e;\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Supported Font Formats\n\nExpo SDK officially supports OTF and TTF font formats across Android, iOS, and web platforms.[2] Place font files in your &#96;assets/fonts&#96; directory by convention.[2]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: install expo modules bare react native 2024 expo-font useFonts"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}

{
  "markdown": "import { loadAsync } from 'expo-font';\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\n\n/**\n * Load a map of custom fonts to use in textual elements.\n * The map keys are used as font names, and can be used with &#96;fontFamily:;&#96;.\n * It returns a boolean describing if all fonts are loaded.\n *\n * Note, the fonts are not \"reloaded\" when you dynamically change the font map.\n *\n * @see https://docs.expo.io/versions/latest/sdk/font/\n * @example const [loaded, error] = useFonts(...);\n */\nexport function useFonts(map) {\n const [loaded, setLoaded] = useState(false);\n const [error, setError] = useState(null);\n\n useEffect(() =\u003e {\n loadAsync(map)\n .then(() =\u003e setLoaded(true))\n .catch(setError);\n }, []);\n\n return [loaded, error];\n}\n",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/expo/google-fonts/main/font-packages/manrope/useFonts.js"
}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Google Fonts Typography",
  "overview": "Add runtime-loaded Manrope and Inter via &#96;expo-font&#96; and &#96;@expo-google-fonts/*&#96; (the maintained stack behind Google Fonts in React Native), install Expo modules in the bare CLI project, centralize real &#96;fontFamily&#96; names and semantic tokens in [src/theme/typography.ts](StreamList/src/theme/typography.ts), gate the app on font loading, and add a small sample component demonstrating usage—without changing every screen because they already spread &#96;typography&#96; into &#96;StyleSheet&#96;s.",
  "plan": "# Google Fonts + centralized typography (StreamList)\n\n## Context\n\n- Typography is already centralized in [StreamList/src/theme/typography.ts](StreamList/src/theme/typography.ts) and consumed via &#96;StyleSheet&#96; + &#96;...typography['token']&#96; across the app (grep shows wide usage). **No inline font styles** is already the dominant pattern.\n- The file is **TypeScript** (&#96;.ts&#96;), not &#96;typography.js&#96;; the deliverable should remain &#96;typography.ts&#96; to match the project and imports.\n- The npm package **&#96;react-native-google-fonts&#96; is not suitable for real use** (version &#96;0.0.0&#96;, stub dependency on &#96;expo-google-fonts&#96;). The maintained approach—used by the Expo team’s [google-fonts](https://github.com/expo/google-fonts) repo—is:\n  - **&#96;expo-font&#96;** (&#96;useFonts&#96; / &#96;loadAsync&#96;)\n  - **&#96;@expo-google-fonts/manrope&#96;** and **&#96;@expo-google-fonts/inter&#96;** (each weight exports a &#96;require()&#96;’d &#96;.ttf&#96; and registers under a predictable name like &#96;Manrope_700Bold&#96;).\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  useAppFonts[\"useAppFonts hook\"]\n  expoFont[\"expo-font useFonts\"]\n  ttf[\"TTF assets from @expo-google-fonts\"]\n  app[\"App.tsx gates UI\"]\n  theme[\"typography.ts fontFamily keys\"]\n  useAppFonts --\u003e expoFont\n  ttf --\u003e expoFont\n  expoFont --\u003e app\n  theme --\u003e screens[\"Screens / StyleSheets\"]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## 1. Native prerequisites: Expo modules (bare RN)\n\nTo use &#96;expo-font&#96;, add Expo modules to this React Native CLI project per [Expo docs — Installing Expo modules in an existing React Native project](https://docs.expo.dev/bare/installing-expo-modules/):\n\n- Run **&#96;npx install-expo-modules@latest&#96;** at the StreamList app root (updates iOS/Android entry points, Podfile integration, etc.).\n- Then **&#96;cd ios \u0026\u0026 pod install&#96;** (or let the CLI prompt handle it).\n\nThis is a one-time native integration step; it is required for &#96;expo-font&#96; on bare RN.\n\n## 2. Dependencies\n\nAdd (versions aligned with your Expo/RN combo via &#96;npx expo install&#96; when available):\n\n- &#96;expo-font&#96;\n- &#96;@expo-google-fonts/manrope&#96;\n- &#96;@expo-google-fonts/inter&#96;\n\nUpdate [StreamList/package.json](StreamList/package.json) and lockfile accordingly.\n\n## 3. Font loading (single place)\n\n- Add **&#96;src/hooks/useAppFonts.ts&#96;** that:\n  - Imports **&#96;useFonts&#96; from &#96;expo-font&#96;** (not a screen; hooks stay in &#96;src/hooks/&#96; per project rules).\n  - Imports only the **font files you need** from the two packages, e.g.:\n    - Manrope: &#96;Manrope_600SemiBold&#96;, &#96;Manrope_700Bold&#96;, &#96;Manrope_800ExtraBold&#96; (maps to current 600/700/800 usage)\n    - Inter: &#96;Inter_400Regular&#96;, &#96;Inter_600SemiBold&#96; (maps to current 400/600 usage)\n  - Passes them into **&#96;useFonts({ ... })&#96;** as one merged map (keys become the **&#96;fontFamily&#96; strings** in RN).\n  - Returns &#96;{ fontsLoaded, fontError }&#96; (or similar) for &#96;App.tsx&#96;.\n\n- Update **[StreamList/App.tsx](StreamList/App.tsx)** to:\n  - Call &#96;useAppFonts()&#96;.\n  - While fonts are not loaded and there is no error, return **&#96;null&#96;** (or a minimal full-screen placeholder **without new hardcoded colors**—prefer &#96;null&#96; to stay minimal).\n  - On error, optionally log or show a minimal fallback; keep behavior simple.\n\nThis ensures fonts are loaded **before** &#96;RootNavigator&#96; and any &#96;Text&#96; render.\n\n## 4. Centralized typography configuration\n\nRefactor **[StreamList/src/theme/typography.ts](StreamList/src/theme/typography.ts)** to:\n\n1. **&#96;fontFamilies&#96;** (or nested &#96;fontFamilies.manrope.*&#96; / &#96;fontFamilies.inter.*&#96;) exporting the **exact registered names** from &#96;useFonts&#96; (e.g. &#96;'Manrope_700Bold'&#96;), so tokens do not repeat magic strings.\n\n2. **Semantic roles** (your request): add clear names such as &#96;heading&#96;, &#96;subheading&#96;, &#96;body&#96;, &#96;caption&#96; (and optionally &#96;overline&#96; for uppercase tracked labels), each built from &#96;fontFamilies&#96; + &#96;fontSize&#96; / &#96;letterSpacing&#96; from [StreamList/src/theme/spacing.ts](StreamList/src/theme/spacing.ts) only where spacing is involved—**font sizes stay in typography** per existing pattern.\n\n3. **Preserve existing tokens** (&#96;display-lg&#96;, &#96;body-md&#96;, &#96;brand-wordmark&#96;, etc.) by redefining them to use the new &#96;fontFamilies&#96; entries and **removing reliance on &#96;fontWeight&#96; for custom fonts** (use the correct face per weight; this avoids Android weight + family quirks).\n\n4. Export a **&#96;Typography&#96; / &#96;TypographyToken&#96; type** (&#96;keyof typeof typography&#96;) for reuse.\n\n**Note:** After this change, components that only spread existing keys should **keep working** without edits, because the public token names stay the same.\n\n## 5. Global usage\n\n- **React Native has no CSS-like global &#96;Text&#96; defaults** that are reliable across versions; your app already enforces consistency by **importing &#96;typography&#96; in &#96;StyleSheet&#96;s**.\n- Optional (small, scalable addition): add **&#96;src/components/common/AppText.tsx&#96;** with a &#96;variant: TypographyToken&#96; prop that applies &#96;typography[variant]&#96;, so new UI avoids duplicating style composition. This is optional; the **sample component** below can use either &#96;AppText&#96; or plain &#96;Text&#96; + styles.\n\n## 6. Example component\n\n- Add **&#96;src/components/common/TypographyExample.tsx&#96;** (or similar name) that imports &#96;typography&#96; and renders a few &#96;Text&#96; nodes showing **heading / subheading / body / caption** (and one existing token like &#96;brand-wordmark&#96;) using **&#96;StyleSheet.create&#96;**. It is not wired into navigation unless you want it for dev—exporting the component is enough to satisfy “example usage.”\n\n## 7. Verification\n\n- Run **&#96;yarn ios&#96; / &#96;yarn android&#96;** after &#96;pod install&#96; and confirm no red screen and that **Manrope/Inter** render (not system font).\n- Run **&#96;yarn lint&#96;** / **&#96;tsc&#96;** as you normally do for this repo.\n\n## Files to touch (expected)\n\n| Area | File |\n|------|------|\n| Native / deps | &#96;package.json&#96;, &#96;yarn.lock&#96;, iOS/Android project files from &#96;install-expo-modules&#96; |\n| Font hook | New &#96;src/hooks/useAppFonts.ts&#96; |\n| App gate | &#96;App.tsx&#96; |\n| Theme | &#96;src/theme/typography.ts&#96; |\n| Example | New &#96;src/components/common/TypographyExample.tsx&#96; |\n| Optional | New &#96;src/components/common/AppText.tsx&#96; |\n\n## Out of scope (minimal change)\n\n- Rewriting every screen to a new API: **not required** if &#96;typography&#96; tokens stay stable.\n- Adding &#96;typography.js&#96;: keep **&#96;typography.ts&#96;** unless you explicitly rename the whole codebase to JS.\n",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Run install-expo-modules and add expo-font + @expo-google-fonts/manrope + @expo-google-fonts/inter; pod install iOS",
      "id": "expo-modules",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Implement src/hooks/useAppFonts.ts and gate App.tsx on fontsLoaded",
      "id": "use-app-fonts",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Refactor src/theme/typography.ts: fontFamilies, semantic tokens (heading/body/caption), preserve existing keys with real fontFamily names",
      "id": "typography-theme",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Add TypographyExample.tsx (optional AppText.tsx) demonstrating token usage",
      "id": "example-component",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [How to add custom fonts in React Native - LogRocket Blog](https://blog.logrocket.com/adding-custom-fonts-react-native/)\n2. [How to use Custom Fonts in React Native CLI | by Usman | Medium](https://medium.com/@usmanmarkaz126/how-to-use-custom-fonts-in-react-native-cli-5e7c9d29ddf1)\n3. [A Guide on Using Custom Fonts in React Native](https://makeuseof.com/react-native-custom-fonts-usage-guide/)\n4. [How to Add Custom Fonts in React Native (iOS \u0026 Android Guide) - TecNimbus](https://tecnimbus.com/how-to-add-custom-fonts-in-react-native-ios-android-guide/)\n5. [Custom Fonts In React Native: Pro Tip! - DEV Community](https://dev.to/mitchiemt11/custom-fonts-in-react-native-pro-tip-4693)\n\n# Adding Custom Google Fonts to React Native CLI Projects\n\n## Setup Steps\n\n**1. Create fonts directory and download fonts**\nCreate an &#96;assets/fonts&#96; folder in your project root and download TTF files from Google Fonts.[1] Extract the downloaded ZIP file and find the TTF files in the static folder.[1]\n\n**2. Create react-native.config.js**\nCreate a &#96;react-native.config.js&#96; file at your project root with this configuration:[2]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nmodule.exports = {\n  project: {\n    ios: {},\n    android: {}\n  },\n  assets: ['./assets/fonts/'],\n};\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**3. Link the fonts**\nRun the following command to link fonts to your native projects:[1][2]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nnpx react-native-asset\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nThis command works for React Native 0.69+. For older versions, use &#96;npx react-native link&#96; instead.[3]\n\n## Using the Fonts\n\nReference the exact font name (the PostScript name, not the filename) in your &#96;fontFamily&#96; style property:[4]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cText style={{ fontFamily: 'Font Name', fontSize: 20 }}\u003eHello\u003c/Text\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Finding the correct font name:** On macOS, open the font file in Font Book and look at the name displayed at the top to get the PostScript name you need.[4]\n\n## Important Notes\n\n- Ensure the font filename matches the font family's full name for cross-platform compatibility.[5]\n- The &#96;fontFamily&#96; name you use in code may differ from the actual filename.[4]\n- After linking, verify fonts are registered in &#96;ios/YourProject/Info.plist&#96; on iOS.[4]",

_**Agent (default)**_


Here is what changed in the plan based on your preference (**React Native CLI, no Expo, no `expo-font`**).

### What you asked for vs what exists on npm

- **`react-native-google-fonts` on npm** is not a real font solution for bare RN (it’s an old **v0.0.0** stub). There isn’t a meaningful `npm install` that “loads Google Fonts” for CLI the way Expo does.
- For **Google Fonts without Expo**, the standard approach is: **download Manrope and Inter `.ttf` files from [Google Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/)**, put them in something like **`assets/fonts/`**, wire **`react-native.config.js`**, run **`npx react-native-asset`**, then use the correct **`fontFamily`** strings in **`typography.ts`**. Fonts are **bundled at build time**, so you **do not** need `useFonts` or an app splash gate.

### Plan updates (saved)

The plan file was rewritten to:

- Remove **Expo modules**, **`expo-font`**, and **`@expo-google-fonts/*`**.
- Add todos for **font assets + linking** and **typography refactor** (no separate “useAppFonts” todo).
- Document honestly that **`react-native-google-fonts` the package** should not be relied on; the workflow is **Google Fonts files + RN asset linking**.

You can read the full updated plan here: [google_fonts_typography_17b37f77.plan.md](/Users/mohdnaeem/.cursor/plans/google_fonts_typography_17b37f77.plan.md).

When you want this implemented in the repo, say **go ahead and implement** (or similar) and we can execute against this revised plan.

---

_**User**_

Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself.

To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.

---
